title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon)
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make delicious Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) with our easy recipe. Crispy, savory, and packed with fresh seafood – perfect for a taste of Korea!
longTailKeywords: how to make Korean seafood pancake
seo.h1: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon)?
Step-by-Step: How to Make Korean Seafood Pancake
Tips for the Best Haemul Pajeon
Variations and Substitutions for Seafood Pancakes
seo.imageAlt: Crispy Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) with shrimp, squid, and green onions, served with a soy dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a homemade Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon), a savory and delightful dish perfect as an appetizer or light meal.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, celebrated for its crispy edges and tender, flavorful interior. This delightful dish combines a light, airy batter with an abundance of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions, creating a perfect balance of textures and tastes. It is often enjoyed as an appetizer or a hearty snack, especially on a rainy day.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: Combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, toasted sesame seeds, minced garlic, and gochugaru (if using) in a small bowl. Stir well and set aside. This savory and slightly tangy sauce is perfect for complementing the rich flavors of the pancake.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Pat the shrimp and squid dry with paper towels to prevent excess moisture. Slice the squid into bite-sized rings. Cut the green onions into approximately 3-inch lengths. Thinly slice the yellow onion and the red chili, if you are using it for a touch of heat and color.

instructions.title: Mix the Pancake Batter
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, baking powder, and salt until well combined. In a separate bowl, lightly whisk the large egg with the cold water. Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, mixing gently until the batter is just combined. Avoid overmixing; a few small lumps are perfectly fine and contribute to a light texture.

instructions.title: Assemble the First Pancake
instructions.text: Heat 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat until shimmering. Pour about 1/2 cup of the prepared batter into the hot skillet, spreading it thinly to form a round pancake. Immediately arrange a portion of the shrimp, squid, green onions, yellow onion, and red chili (if using) evenly over the wet batter.

instructions.title: Cook the Pancake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Cook the pancake for 3-4 minutes on one side until the bottom is beautifully golden brown and crispy. Drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake, then carefully flip it using a large spatula. Continue to cook for another 3-4 minutes on the second side until it is also golden brown and the seafood is fully cooked through.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a serving plate. Repeat the process with the remaining batter and ingredients, adding more oil to the pan as needed for each pancake. Cut the finished Haemul Pajeon into wedges and serve them immediately with the prepared dipping sauce for the best taste and texture.

tips: For extra crispiness, ensure your skillet is very hot before adding the batter and do not overcrowd the pan. Using a combination of all-purpose and rice flour also enhances crispiness.
Feel free to customize the seafood with other fresh options like clams, oysters, or even mussels. Just ensure all seafood is fresh and cooked through.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking to enjoy its best crispy texture. Pair it with a cold glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine) for an authentic Korean experience.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the vibrant streets and cozy homes of Korea with one of its most beloved comfort foods: Haemul Pajeon. This savory, crispy, and utterly delicious Korean seafood pancake is more than just a dish; it's an experience, a perfect blend of fresh ocean flavors, aromatic scallions, and a golden-brown, satisfyingly chewy yet crisp batter. Whether you're seeking an exciting appetizer, a hearty snack, or a delightful accompaniment to your favorite Korean BBQ, Haemul Pajeon promises to deliver a symphony of tastes and textures that will leave you craving more.

## What is Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon)?

Haemul Pajeon (pronounced "Hae-mul Pah-jeon") directly translates to "seafood scallion pancake." It's a popular variety of jeon, a general term for Korean pan-fried dishes made with various ingredients coated in a flour batter and egg wash. What sets Haemul Pajeon apart is its generous inclusion of fresh seafood and an abundance of green onions, which are the foundational elements providing its distinct flavor and texture.

This savory Korean Seafood Pancake isn't just a restaurant staple; it’s a dish deeply embedded in Korean culture, often enjoyed on rainy days with a glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or cold beer. It’s a dish that evokes warmth, comfort, and conviviality. Unlike thinner, more delicate pancakes, Haemul Pajeon is typically thick and substantial, boasting a crispy exterior from pan-frying and a soft, chewy interior packed with tender seafood and vibrant scallions. It’s a testament to simple ingredients coming together to create something truly extraordinary.

## Ingredients for Your Haemul Pajeon

To create your perfect Korean Seafood Pancake, you'll need a combination of fresh ingredients and pantry staples. The beauty of this dish lies in its flexibility, allowing you to customize the seafood to your preference.

For the Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour (or a mix of all-purpose and rice flour for extra crispiness)
*   1 large egg
*   1 cup cold water or anchovy broth (broth adds depth of flavor)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   Pinch of black pepper

For the Filling:
*   1 bunch (about 8-10 stalks) green onions (scallions), cut into 2-3 inch pieces
*   1/2 lb mixed seafood, such as:
    *   Small shrimp (peeled and deveined)
    *   Squid (cleaned and sliced into rings or bite-sized pieces)
    *   Mussels (cleaned)
    *   Oysters (optional)
    *   Bay scallops (optional)
*   1/2 small onion, thinly sliced (optional)
*   1 small red or green chili pepper, thinly sliced (optional, for a hint of heat)
*   Vegetable oil or canola oil, for frying

For the Dipping Sauce (optional, but highly recommended):
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) or a pinch of red pepper flakes
*   1 clove garlic, minced
*   1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1 teaspoon chopped green onion (white part)

## Step-by-Step: How to Make Korean Seafood Pancake

Mastering the art of making a delicious Korean Seafood Pancake is simpler than you might think. Follow these steps for a perfect result every time:

1.  **Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables**: If using frozen seafood, thaw it completely and pat it dry with paper towels. Cut larger pieces of seafood into bite-sized portions. Wash and trim the green onions, then cut them into 2-3 inch lengths. Thinly slice the optional onion and chili pepper.
2.  **Make the Batter**: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, and pepper. In a separate bowl, lightly beat the egg, then add it to the dry ingredients along with the cold water or broth. Whisk until just combined. The batter should be slightly thick but pourable, similar to pancake batter. Avoid overmixing, as this can lead to a tough pancake. A few lumps are fine.
3.  **Combine Ingredients**: Add the prepared seafood, most of the green onions (reserve a handful for the top), and the optional sliced onion and chili pepper to the batter. Gently fold everything together until the seafood and vegetables are evenly coated.
4.  **Heat the Pan**: Heat a large (10-12 inch) non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of cooking oil, ensuring the entire surface of the pan is coated. The oil should be shimmering but not smoking.
5.  **Cook the Pancake**: Pour about half of the batter mixture into the hot pan, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake about 1/2 to 3/4 inch thick. Immediately arrange the reserved green onion pieces artfully on top of the pancake, pressing them gently into the batter.
6.  **Flip and Finish**: Cook for about 5-7 minutes on the first side, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy and the edges begin to set. Drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake to encourage crispiness. Using a wide spatula, carefully flip the pancake. Cook for another 5-7 minutes on the second side, until golden brown, crispy, and the seafood is fully cooked through. If the pancake seems to be browning too quickly, reduce the heat slightly.
7.  **Serve**: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Slice it into wedges or squares, similar to pizza. Repeat with the remaining batter, adding more oil to the pan as needed.

## Tips for the Best Haemul Pajeon

Achieving the ultimate Korean Seafood Pancake involves a few key techniques:

*   **Batter Consistency is Key**: The batter should be thick enough to hold the ingredients together but thin enough to spread easily. If it’s too thick, add a tablespoon of water at a time; if too thin, a tablespoon of flour.
*   **Don't Skimp on Oil**: A generous amount of oil is crucial for achieving that characteristic crispy exterior. Don't be afraid to add a little extra around the edges while cooking.
*   **High Heat Start**: Begin cooking on medium-high heat to get a good sear and crispness. You can reduce the heat to medium once the first side is golden to ensure the interior cooks through without burning the outside.
*   **Plenty of Scallions**: Green onions are not just a filler; they are a star ingredient. Their fresh, slightly pungent flavor cuts through the richness of the seafood and batter.
*   **Pat Seafood Dry**: Excess moisture from seafood can make your pancake soggy. Patting it thoroughly dry before adding it to the batter helps ensure crispiness.
*   **Cook in Batches**: Don't overcrowd your pan. If you're making a large batch, cook one or two pancakes at a time to maintain consistent heat and crispiness.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Pajeon

Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed hot off the pan, when its crispy edges and tender interior are at their peak.

*   **Dipping Sauce**: The accompanying dipping sauce is non-negotiable! The tangy, savory, and slightly spicy sauce perfectly complements the rich flavors of the pancake. Mix the ingredients listed above for a classic version, or adjust to your taste.
*   **Beverage Pairings**: In Korea, this dish is famously paired with makgeolli (a milky rice wine) or a cold beer, especially on a rainy day. The effervescence of these drinks cuts through the richness of the pancake beautifully.
*   **As an Appetizer or Side**: Serve sliced Haemul Pajeon as an appetizer for a Korean feast, or as a banchan (side dish) alongside a hearty main course like bulgogi or bibimbap.
*   **Light Meal**: For a quick and satisfying meal, a large Korean Seafood Pancake with rice and kimchi is often all you need.

## Variations and Substitutions for Seafood Pancakes

While the classic Korean Seafood Pancake is a masterpiece, there are many ways to adapt it to your taste or dietary needs:

*   **Different Seafood Combinations**: Feel free to use only shrimp, only squid, or a mix of whatever fresh seafood is available to you. Clams, small pieces of firm white fish, or even imitation crab meat can work.
*   **Vegetarian Pajeon**: For a meat-free option, omit the seafood entirely. You can make a delicious Kimchi Pajeon (Kimchi pancake), Buchu Jeon (Korean chive pancake), or even add other vegetables like mushrooms, zucchini, or carrots.
*   **Add Kimchi**: For a spicy, tangy kick, add a handful of chopped, well-squeezed kimchi to your batter. Kimchi Pajeon is another incredibly popular variation.
*   **Spice It Up**: Incorporate gochujang (Korean chili paste) into your batter for a deeper, spicier flavor profile.
*   **Flour Alternatives**: While all-purpose flour is common, using a specific Korean pancake mix (pajeon garu) or adding rice flour can enhance the crispiness. Gluten-free flour blends can also be used, though the texture might vary slightly.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a dish; it's a celebration of flavors, textures, and Korean culinary tradition. Its versatility and comforting appeal make it a favorite for many. So, gather your ingredients, fire up your pan, and get ready to create your own irresistible Korean Seafood Pancake. It's a rewarding experience that promises delicious results, bringing a taste of Korea right into your kitchen. Enjoy the crispy, savory goodness!
